result:
Wind makes messages in the field’s tall grass, sentences no one will transcribe. When they come, combine harvesters will erase all script, but before that moment the meadow keeps talking, a slow murmur of seed-heads rubbing together, gossip that began with glaciers. Last night I borrowed a pocket of this language and pinned it inside my coat like contraband; now it rattles against my ribs, a discreet clattering of possible futures.  

Town smells of bread crust and exhaust. The library hums like a beekeeper’s veil, all those wings vibrating against paper and the thin patience of silence. I return overdue books to their gaps, satisfied to witness shelves reunite, yet frustrated because memory refuses an identical tidy shelf inside my skull where chapters might stand to attention. Instead, sentences migrate—sometimes Shelley to the gardening tome, sometimes a menu to the end-times report.  

Coffee shops invent countries. Today: Isle of Steamed Milk, Republic of Cinnamon, Dictatorship of Kindly Stamps on Loyalty Cards. Citizens approach the counter speaking currencies that dissolve on the tongue. The barista wears galaxies on her apron; every splash is another Big Bang.  

Birds commute like punctuation marks across the sky, ending thoughts none of us will finish. I walk below them, an accent mark wobbling between syllables of pavement. Carrying nothing visible, still I arrive heavier, paragraphs attaching themselves to my shoes, italic mud clinging until tomorrow’s inevitable rinse cycle of rain. In the meantime, the meadow continues its invisible news.

prompt: Write freely about whatever you want for 250 words.
